findings.detail: _deserialize_state uses .get() with defaults for all fields. _deserialize_gate gracefully handles missing/corrupt data (None for missing gate, False for invalid completed). Old session files without new fields load correctly.
Only stdlib imports (json, logging, os, dataclasses, datetime, pathlib, typing). No marketmind imports at all. Clean leaf module.

SessionState → _serialize_state (dict) → JSON → _deserialize_state → SessionState. All fields round-tripped: session_id, mode, current_gate, gate1-3 (GateCheckpoint with completed/timestamp/data), started_at, last_activity. GateCheckpoint.data uses dict[str, Any] with json.dumps(default=str) for non-standard types. Lossless for JSON-serializable types.

save() writes .tmp then tmp.replace(filepath). If crash mid-write: .tmp is incomplete but load() only reads {session_id}.json, not .tmp. list_sessions() globs *.json, so .tmp excluded. Next save() overwrites stale .tmp. Safe.

notes: All checks pass. Clean module with robust serialization and crash safety.
